Title: Pyrophosphate-fructose 6-phosphate 1-phosphotransferase (PFP1) regulates starch biosynthesis and seed development via heterotetramer formation in rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms heterotetrameric PFP complex with PFP1b that supports starch biosynthesis-related carbon metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate for seed quality
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression significantly increased in pfp1-3 mutant relative to wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PFP1a1 physically interacts with PFP1b in planta. 2. PFP1a1 also forms homodimers and heterodimers with other PFP1 subunits. 3. Its transcript level is significantly increased in the pfp1-3 mutant, suggesting compensatory or complex-related response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper describes PFP1a proteins as putative alpha-subunits participating in heterotetramer formation; not directly mapped by mutant phenotype alone
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; BiFC; Co-IP; RNA-seq context; accession NA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana for interaction assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner gene for engineering PFP complex activity in starch metabolism

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms heterotetrameric PFP complex with PFP1b contributing to carbon flux for starch biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage carbohydrate determining endosperm quality and grain filling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression significantly increased in pfp1-3 mutant relative to wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PFP1a2 physically interacts with PFP1b in planta. 2. PFP1a2 forms homodimers and heterodimers with PFP1 subunits. 3. Transcript abundance increases in the pfp1-3 mutant, consistent with a compensatory response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of three alpha-subunits able to bind PFP1b; not directly shown as independent causal gene for phenotype
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; BiFC; Co-IP; RNA-seq context; accession NA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana for interaction assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complex partner for modifying PFP activity and starch traits

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with PFP1b as part of heterotetrameric PFP complex associated with starch metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage carbohydrate determining endosperm quality and grain filling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression significantly increased in pfp1-3 mutant relative to wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PFP1a4 physically interacts with PFP1b in planta. 2. Unlike PFP1a1 and PFP1a2, PFP1a4 did not show homodimerization. 3. Its transcript level is significantly increased in the pfp1-3 mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms interaction with PFP1b but not homodimer; included because heterotetramer formation is a central mechanistic result
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; BiFC; Co-IP; RNA-seq context; accession NA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana for interaction assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for tuning PFP complex composition in metabolic engineering

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and normal grain filling; loss of function causes floury endosperm, reduced starch and grain weight, and increased seed protein and lipid content
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly converts fructose 6-phosphate and pyrophosphate to fructose 1,6-bisphosphate and orthophosphate in glycolysis/gluconeogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: glycolytic entry point supplying carbon flux to endosperm starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage carbohydrate determining grain weight, endosperm texture, seed vigor and food quality
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in root, stem and leaf; transcript strongly reduced in pfp1-3 mutant; implicated during developing endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in root, stem, leaf and developing seeds/endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; transgenic complementation; PFP enzyme assay; SEM and starch structural analyses; RNA-seq; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pfp1-3 is a recessive floury-endosperm mutant with slower grain filling, lower 1000-grain weight, reduced yield, reduced starch, and increased protein and lipid content. 2. Map-based cloning identified a splice-site mutation in LOC_Os06g13810 causing premature stop codon; ubiquitin-driven complementation rescued grain and starch-granule phenotypes. 3. PFP1 activity dropped to 6.3% of wild type in the mutant, confirming functional loss. 4. Mutation altered amylopectin structure, reduced starch crystallinity, increased starch digestibility, and caused embryo defects and poor germination. 5. RNA-seq and metabolite profiling showed altered expression of starch biosynthetic genes and accumulation of glycolytic intermediates, trehalose-6-phosphate, and multiple lipid classes, indicating broad metabolic-flux control.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (developing grains at 3, 6, 12 DAP); metabolomics by UHPLC-MS and LC-SI-MS; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural paddy field growth; developing seeds at 3, 6, 12 DAP; germination assay on half-strength MS without sugar
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PFP beta-subunit family; pyrophosphate-dependent phosphofructokinase family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step in primary carbohydrate metabolic flux
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces grain yield and grain weight, impairs embryo development and germination, alters storage composition, and changes starch quality/digestibility
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving grain yield and quality by maintaining or optimizing carbon flux to starch; caution that disruption causes severe seed-quality and germination penalties; may be valuable in gene stacking with other starch-pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although named beta-subunit in this paper, it is described as the regulatory subunit of the heterotetrameric PFP complex in rice; mutation also affects lipid metabolism and trehalose-related signaling metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) used for complementation
